title: The Kato Equation: A Zero-Parameter Exponent Selection Rule for Urban Scaling
description: Working paper on a zero-parameter relay-depth ladder for urban scaling exponents. Tested against the Bettencourt et al. (PNAS 2007) 22-indicator panel: integer relay-depth predictions match 17/22 reported confidence intervals; network-anchored composition lifts coverage to 21/22; a label-free K=2 lower-valley mixture (H in {1,2}) for physical d=3 cities recovers all 22/22, with MAE = 0.031 and RMSE = 0.045. Patents land on H=2; GDP and wages occupy an H=4-5 corridor; infrastructure follows the negative branch. Paper 4 of the Kato Equation Working Paper Cluster (Papers 0-10 plus Manifesto 1/2 forthcoming). Release v1.1 (manuscript V128/V129) polish wave: Manifesto 1 / Manifesto 2 reframe, abstract correction to K=2 lower-valley H in {1,2}, SI cross-time beta table column-collision fix, data and code availability section forward-looking (concept DOI primary).

creators.name: Kato, Shota
creators.affiliation: AI and Future Co., Ltd., Tokyo, Japan
